compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context / Problem

The plan-review `[rebar:<C>]` citation-escape lets a plan cite a symbol a prerequisite ticket
will create; the E4/G1G2/E6 finders are instructed to "retrieve C via the `show_ticket(C)` tool"
and credit the citation only if C's plan establishes the functionality
(`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E4.md:16`, `plan_review_G1G2.md:10`). The agentic finder's
`show_ticket` tool is `rebar.llm.pai_tools.rebar_tools`'s inner `show_ticket`
(`src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py:204`), which forwards its argument verbatim to
`_reads.show_ticket`.

**Defect:** the citation token is literally `[rebar:<id>]`, so the finder passes the token content
**`rebar:<id>` — including the `rebar:` scheme** — to the tool. rebar's id resolver does not
recognize the `rebar:` prefix, so `rebar show 'rebar:<id>'` exits "Ticket not found," the finder
cannot confirm the prerequisite, and the citation-escape **fails closed on a spurious
"cited prerequisite does not exist"** BLOCK. Runtime-proven by an instrumented review: the finder
called `show_ticket('rebar:8153-…')` and `show_ticket('rebar:fc72-…')` (both → "not found") while
the bare-id call `show_ticket('41e2-…')` returned OK. Because the LLM only *sometimes* strips the
prefix itself, the failure is intermittent — which is why some citing plans pass and others block.
This breaks the documented citation mechanism (`docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` "Citing a
prerequisite's symbol").

## Approach

Normalize the agent-supplied ticket ref at the tool boundary — the single site where a
`rebar:`-scheme ref originates. Add `_normalize_ticket_ref()` to `pai_tools.py` that strips a
leading `rebar:` (case-insensitive) plus any wrapping `[]`/whitespace, and call it in both
`show_ticket` and `comment_ticket` before resolving. Stripping is unambiguous and safe: no ticket
id (hex groups `[a-z0-9]{4}-…`) or alias (hyphenated words) contains a colon, so a leading
`rebar:` is never part of a real id; the function is idempotent on a bare id.

**Rejected alternative.** Fixing it in the reviewer prompts (tell the LLM to strip the scheme) —
probabilistic (LLM may still forget) versus a deterministic tool-boundary normalization. Rejected
widening `_reads.show_ticket` / the CLI id-resolver — the `rebar:` scheme only ever arrives from
the LLM citation-token path, so normalizing at that boundary keeps CLI/library id semantics
untouched.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py` — add `_normalize_ticket_ref`; apply in `show_ticket` and
  `comment_ticket`.
- `tests/interfaces/store/test_scoped_ticket_tool.py` — add a RED-first case pinning that the tool
  resolves a `rebar:<id>`-form ref.

## Testing

- Happy path (the defect): `show_ticket(f"rebar:{tid}")` resolves to the same ticket as the bare
  `tid` — returns the ticket JSON (title + id present), not `Error: … not found`. Fails RED against
  the current tool; passes after the strip.
- Parity: the existing `test_show_ticket_tool_reads` (bare id) and `test_comment_ticket_tool_comments`
  still pass — a bare id is unchanged (idempotent normalization).

Tier: unit (interfaces/store) — builds a real store via the `rebar_repo` fixture, creates a ticket,
invokes the actual `rebar_tools` `show_ticket` function; no LLM.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The agentic `show_ticket` tool resolves a `rebar:<id>`-scheme ref to the same ticket as the
      bare id; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/interfaces/store/test_scoped_ticket_tool.py -k rebar_scheme_prefix`
      (the new case passes).
- [ ] Bare-id reads and comments are unchanged (parity); verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/interfaces/store/test_scoped_ticket_tool.py`
      (the full file passes, including the pre-existing read/comment cases).

file_impact.reason: Strip rebar: citation scheme prefix in show_ticket/comment_ticket tools

compiled_state.title: Plan-review show_ticket tool rejects the rebar: citation-scheme prefix, breaking the [rebar:<C>] escape
